changes_made.description: Baseline run with original verbose skill (copied from skills/sql/SKILL.md). sql-migrate scored 100, sql-query scored 77 due to incorrect JSON formatting (top_customer as object instead of string, product_ranking as array of objects instead of array of strings).

Over-optimized skill with heavy Python-based JSON generation approach. Trimmed PostgreSQL/MySQL content. sql-migrate scored 100 but sql-query dropped to 30 — model stopped after 2 tool calls without producing query_results.json. The Python-heavy approach confused the model.

Reverted to balanced approach: kept SQLite-focused content with clear examples, added explicit JSON output formatting rules (scalar vs array of strings vs array of objects vs mapping), added normalization pattern for sql-migrate task, removed PostgreSQL/MySQL sections. Both tasks scored 100.
Confirmation run with same skill. Both tasks scored 100, confirming consistency.

final_skill_changes_summary: Replaced verbose multi-database skill with SQLite-focused version. Key additions: (1) explicit JSON output formatting rules distinguishing scalars, string arrays, object arrays, and mappings; (2) denormalized CSV normalization pattern with staging table approach; (3) common query patterns for revenue calculation, ranking with window functions, monthly grouping. Removed: PostgreSQL, MySQL sections, backup/restore for non-SQLite databases.
